author_title_or_source: Lin/NIST fitting-loss measurement methods and state-of-the-art review
fluid_geometry_regime: single-phase fittings; pressure taps; geometry-dependent fitting data
key_model_or_equation: separate static pressure, kinetic pressure, straight-pipe friction, velocity basis, and recovery before reporting K
required_ranges_or_metadata: tap/plane locations; pressure basis; velocity basis; geometry; Re; recovery status
tamu_overlap: directly relevant as extraction method, not as TAMU coefficient source
implementation_use: basis for section_K, cluster_K, component_K naming and pressure-corner audits
failure_criterion: reject universal component K if component isolation or recovery is not demonstrated

author_title_or_source: Csizmadia and Hos corrected element pressure-drop decomposition
key_model_or_equation: delta_p = delta_p_all - delta_p_in - delta_p_out; zeta normalized by inlet dynamic pressure
required_ranges_or_metadata: attached-pipe corrections; inlet velocity basis; element domain
tamu_overlap: directly relevant to CFD section extraction
implementation_use: supports subtracting upstream/downstream straight losses before cluster or component coefficient
failure_criterion: reject component K if upstream/downstream disturbances are intentionally retained

author_title_or_source: Patino-Jaramillo, Iglesias, Vera low-Re planar tee pressure-loss coefficients
fluid_geometry_regime: steady laminar planar 90-degree tee/sharp elbow; constant-property; reduced-static pressure
key_model_or_equation: Kprime(mdot_split, Re3, alpha) with reduced hydrostatic pressure basis
required_ranges_or_metadata: tee approx 20<=Re3<=200, 1<=alpha<=3, 0<=mdot1<=1; planar geometry; source-defined velocity basis
tamu_overlap: physics relevant to low-Re junction dependence, not geometry-matched to circular TAMU corners
implementation_use: candidate source-bounded split/path coefficient shape and recirculation-map precedent
failure_criterion: reject as universal circular-pipe dynamic K or as admitted TAMU coefficient without geometry/basis conversion

author_title_or_source: Patino-Jaramillo, Iglesias, Vera planar tee flow fields and recirculation maps
fluid_geometry_regime: steady laminar planar tee flow fields
key_model_or_equation: recirculation zone count/location as functions of Re3, alpha, and mass split
required_ranges_or_metadata: 0<Re3<=200 flow-map simulations; planar geometry; split ratio required
tamu_overlap: relevant to flags and topology, not direct threshold source
failure_criterion: reject universal recirculation threshold or one-stream tee closure when TAMU fields show persistent exchange

author_title_or_source: Al-Tameemi and Ricco 90-degree sharp miter elbow K(Re)
required_ranges_or_metadata: 500<Re<60000; miter geometry and velocity basis required
tamu_overlap: useful to prevent Re-independent bend-K assumptions; likely not TAMU geometry matched
implementation_use: screening/sensitivity only unless TAMU bend is comparable
failure_criterion: reject direct use for smooth bends, circular elbows, clusters, recirculating corners, or unmatched Re

author_title_or_source: Salehi, Idem, Sleiti close-coupled elbow cluster loss
